## Overview

**v0.5.0 — Linux support arrives.** TildaZ is now a cross-platform Quake-style
drop-down terminal on **Windows, macOS, and Linux (Wayland)**, plus a round of
cross-platform polish.

The Linux host is a direct Wayland client — no GTK or Qt dependency — with a
true drop-down on `wlr-layer-shell` compositors and a per-desktop drop-down +
global-hotkey path for the major desktops. Compared to the `v0.5.0-rc` series,
this final release adds COSMIC, Hyprland and sway hotkey integration, the GNOME
and Cinnamon drop-down extensions, monitor-hotplug recovery, IME scroll parity,
drag-select auto-scroll, and unified cross-platform logging.

No config schema changes since v0.4.3.

## Desktop support (Linux)

| Desktop / compositor | Drop-down | Global hotkey | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| **KDE Plasma 6 (KWin)** | `wlr-layer-shell` | XDG portal `GlobalShortcuts` + `KGlobalAccel` takeover | ✅ Verified (incl. 125% / 150% / 170% fractional scaling) |
| **Hyprland** (wlroots) | `wlr-layer-shell` | compositor bind → `tildaz --toggle` (install.sh writes Lua/conf) | ✅ Verified (0.55.4) |
| **sway** (wlroots) | `wlr-layer-shell` | `bindsym` via i3-ipc → `tildaz --toggle` | ✅ Verified |
| **Cinnamon** (Wayland) | drop-down extension | `gsettings` custom keybinding | ✅ Verified (+ fcitx5-hangul IME) |
| **GNOME** (mutter, Wayland) | drop-down extension (`xdg-shell`) | `gsettings` custom keybinding | ✅ Verified (GNOME Shell 48) |
| **COSMIC** | `wlr-layer-shell` | RON custom shortcut → `tildaz --toggle` | ✅ Verified |

On compositors without `wlr-layer-shell` (mutter), TildaZ falls back to a normal
`xdg-shell` window; on GNOME and Cinnamon a desktop extension restores the
drop-down placement.

## Downloads

| Platform | File | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Windows 10/11 x64 | `tildaz-v0.5.0-win-x64.zip` | Unzip anywhere. `tildaz.exe` + bundled ConPTY. Unsigned (same SmartScreen / AV handling as before). |
| Windows 11 ARM64 | `tildaz-v0.5.0-win-arm64.zip` | ARM64-native binary + ARM64 ConPTY. Unsigned. |
| macOS 13+ universal | `tildaz-v0.5.0-macos.dmg` | Apple Silicon + Intel. Drag `TildaZ.app` to Applications. Ad-hoc signed. |
| Linux portable (glibc 2.28+) | `tildaz-v0.5.0-linux-{x86_64,aarch64}.tar.gz` | Extract, run `./install.sh` (writes `.desktop` + icon under `~/.local/share/`, no `sudo`). |
| Linux Debian / Ubuntu | `tildaz_0.5.0_{amd64,arm64}.deb` | `sudo dpkg -i tildaz_*.deb` or open with the Software app. |
| Linux Fedora / RHEL / openSUSE | `tildaz-0.5.0-1.{x86_64,aarch64}.rpm` | `sudo dnf install ./tildaz-*.rpm` |
| Linux distro-independent | `TildaZ-0.5.0-{x86_64,aarch64}.AppImage` | `chmod +x` and run. FUSE optional. glibc 2.28+. |
| Arch / Manjaro / EndeavourOS | `tildaz-0.5.0-1-x86_64.pkg.tar.zst` | `sudo pacman -U tildaz-*.pkg.tar.zst` (x86_64 only — Arch's official arch). |

**A running Wayland session (compositor) is required — TildaZ does not run
under X11.** It speaks the Wayland protocol directly over the socket, so it
does not link `libwayland-client`; the only build-time link is `glibc 2.28+`.

The `.deb` / `.rpm` declare the core runtime libraries — `libxkbcommon`,
`libfreetype`, `libfontconfig` (keyboard + fonts) — as hard dependencies.
`libharfbuzz` (ligatures), `libdbus-1` (KDE portal / KGlobalAccel hotkey), and
`libgio-2.0` (GNOME/Cinnamon `gsettings` hotkey), plus the `xdg-desktop-portal`
service, are recommended: all are loaded at runtime via `dlopen` and degrade to
a feature-off state (with a clear log line) when absent, rather than crashing.
The `.tar.gz` and `.AppImage` rely on these libraries already being present in
the desktop session.

## Migration from v0.4.3

None. The config schema is unchanged. Linux reads the same
`~/.config/tildaz/config.json`; default `shell`, `font.family`, and
`font.glyph_fallback` values are Linux-specific (`/bin/bash`, `DejaVu Sans
Mono`, `Noto Sans CJK`, `Noto Color Emoji`).

## What's new since v0.4.3

### Linux Wayland host ([#189](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/189))

A toolkit-free Wayland client. Capabilities are probed at startup and the host
degrades cleanly when a compositor does not advertise a feature.

- **Window / display.** `wlr-layer-shell` drop-down with config-driven
  `dock_position` / `width_percent` / `height_percent` / `offset_percent` /
  `opacity_percent` / `theme`; `wp_fractional_scale_v1` + `wp_viewporter` for
  KDE-style fractional scaling with live logical/physical separation;
  `xdg-shell` fallback on mutter.
- **Input.** Mouse selection drag (with edge auto-scroll), double-click word
  selection, wheel scroll, scrollbar drag, auto-copy on selection, right-click
  paste, `Ctrl+Shift+C/V`, `Shift+PgUp/PgDn`, `Ctrl+Shift+R` screen reset,
  `Alt+F4` (multi-tab quit confirm), `Alt+1..9` tab jump. Client-side key
  repeat follows `wl_keyboard.repeat_info`; OS cursor shape via
  `wp_cursor_shape_v1`.
- **Tabs.** Full multi-session tab bar (`<` `>` `+` `x`, drag-reorder with
  auto-scroll, double-click rename with IME, 32-tab cap). Single-tab mode hides
  the bar so the grid expands ([#127](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/127)).
- **Fonts.** `fontconfig` discovery, `FreeType` rasterization (incl. BGRA color
  emoji), `HarfBuzz` shaping with 2- and 3-character ligature lookahead and a
  grapheme-cluster path (combining marks, VS-16, skin-tone, ZWJ). Block /
  shade glyphs drawn procedurally.
- **IME.** `zwp_text_input_v3` (done/apply batch). Hangul / kanji / hanzi commit
  reaches the PTY; inline pre-edit with the macOS/Windows purple background;
  `Ctrl+key` discards composition; candidate popup anchored via
  `set_cursor_rectangle`. Verified with fcitx5-hangul.

### Per-desktop drop-down + hotkey

- **KDE Plasma 6** — portal `GlobalShortcuts`; on hotkey collision TildaZ
  detects the owner via `org.kde.KGlobalAccel`, asks to confirm takeover, and
  reassigns via `setForeignShortcut` (config is the source of truth)
  ([#207](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/207)).
- **Hyprland** ([#227](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/227)) & **sway**
  ([#226](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/226)) — compositor keybind to
  `tildaz --toggle` over a `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/tildaz.sock` single-instance socket
  ([#198](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/198)); `install.sh` writes the
  bind (Hyprland Lua/conf; sway via i3-ipc).
- **GNOME** ([#228](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/228)) & **Cinnamon**
  ([#229](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/229)) — a Shell/Cinnamon
  extension provides the drop-down placement on mutter; the hotkey is a
  `gsettings` custom keybinding.
- **COSMIC** ([#230](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/230)) — layer-shell
  drop-down + a RON custom shortcut (`tildaz --toggle`).

### Cross-platform

- **Ligature parity** — Windows / macOS now share Linux's `ligature.classify`:
  Fira Code "spacer" sequences, 3-character ligatures (`===`, `<=>`, `||=`), and
  the GPOS `x_offset` fix.
- **Drag-select auto-scroll** ([#245](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/245))
  — dragging a selection past the top/bottom edge scrolls and extends into
  scrollback (all three platforms).
- **OS mouse cursor shape** ([#193](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/193))
  — I-beam in the grid / rename field, arrow elsewhere.
- **IME scroll parity** ([#242](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/242)) —
  typing (and pre-edit) while scrolled back jumps to the bottom.
- **Unified logging** ([#197](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/197)) —
  consistent levels/format across platforms; `--verbose` / `TILDAZ_VERBOSE` for
  protocol-level detail.
- **Windows ARM64** ([#191](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/191)) — first
  official ARM64 build with ARM64-native ConPTY.

### Linux stability

Monitor hotplug recovery ([#241](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/241)),
startup latency 165 ms → 25 ms on KWin
([#205](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/205)), selection-copy and
shutdown leak/deadlock fixes
([#235](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/235),
[#218](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/218),
[#223](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/223)), and fractional-scale dock
edge fixes ([#220](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/220),
[#233](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/233)).

## Known limitations

- **KDE floating dock overlap** ([#206](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/206))
  — a Plasma *floating* panel can be partially covered; this needs a KWin-side
  defloat that layer-shell clients cannot trigger (KDE Bug 491006). Workaround:
  turn off the panel's *Floating* option. Same root cause affects yakuake.
- **KDE Alt+F4 → Cancel flicker** ([#239](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/239))
  — KWin closes the focused layer surface on Alt+F4, so Cancel must recreate it,
  which is briefly visible. Compositor-inherent; OK-to-quit is unaffected.
- **VSCode/Electron (X11) Hangul IME** ([#194](https://github.com/ensky0/tildaz/issues/194))
  — an fcitx5 frontend-separation limitation, not fixable at the protocol level.
- The software `wl_shm` renderer is a placeholder until a GPU path lands.
